g.title: CANOpenRobotController
YAML configuration files used by the different robot platforms to dynamically load some important parameters.
Markdown main documentation. After using Doxigen you can also access the Doxygen documentation in html/index.html.
ROS and ROS2 launch files if using a ROS/ROS2 stateMachine.
External libraries used (and compiled) by CORC. Some are installed as git submodules.
ROS and ROS2 msg files if using a ROS stateMachine.
Set of useful scripts to initiate CAN or virtual CAN interfaces, upload files to BeagleBone or create a new state machine (app).

Source files organised in three levels: 1)apps: contains application code (examples) and is where you can define your main logic; 2) hardware: contains platform (robot) and IO drivers to be modified/added to if you use non-supported hardware or new hardware functionalities; 3)core: with core CORC logic, likely not to be modified.

All the demo/example apps provided with CORC. Each app is a dedicated StateMachine with its own states. See the Get started pages for details. Your custom apps can also be placed in a different, external folder, for easier maintenance.
The very basic app example to start with to test CORC, the compilation process...
The EMU (M3) state machine example.
A template used by the createStateMachine script to ease the creation of a new custom StateMachine (app).
other examples are available, check the Get started pages.
Core code defining the main logic and common functionalities used by all CORC. Should very likely not be modified.
The low-level CANOpen stack. Should very likely not be modified.
Convenience classes to manage network communication via FLNL. Should very likely not be modified.
The base robot and joint classes. Should very likely not be modified.
The core/base StateMachine classes. Should very likely not be modified.
All the different hardware drivers and interaction code, including the various supported robots definition, motor drives and other IOs.
Motor drives classes. Include dedicated classes for Copley, Kinco and EPOS CANOpen based motor drives.
Drivers for various IOs (IMUs, encoders, keyboard...). See dedicated document page on supported IOs.
Classes for each supported platform (robot) and their respective Joints where appropriate. Add or modify only if you need a new platform or new platform functionality (e.g. new sensor...).
Classes for the FIT-HV waist exoskeleton (ULS robotics).
Classes for an example logging robot: only logging state.
Classes for the ArmMotus M1 (Fourier Intelligence).
Classes for the ArmMotus M2 (Fourier Intelligence).
Classes for the ArmMotus M2 Pro (Fourier Intelligence).
Classes for the ArmMotus EMU (Fourier Intelligence).
Classes for the X2 exoskeleton (Fourier Intelligence).
